definitions.text: A measure of cold or heat, often measurable with a thermometer.
examples: The boiling temperature of pure water is 100 degrees Celsius.
The temperature in the room dropped nearly 20 degrees; it went from hot to cold.

definitions.text: An elevated body temperature, as present in many illnesses; fever.
examples: You have a temperature. I think you should stay home today. You’re sick.

definitions.text: A property of macroscopic amounts of matter that serves to gauge the average intensity of the random actual motions of the individually mobile particulate constituents.
